warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Computed Fukui reactivity indices for ferrocene using the chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill at the GFN2-xTB level (xtb backend).  

Method provenance
1. Property: Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), f⁰ (radical attack) and dual descriptor for each of the 21 atoms; headline value reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ found (on the Fe centre).
2. Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semi-empirical extended tight-binding); no Gaussian basis set; density-functional tight-binding formalism.
3. Geometry provenance: single-point evaluation on the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz coordinates (no further optimization performed).
4. Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
5. Charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral reference; cation (+1, doublet) and anion (–1, doublet) states generated internally for finite-difference Fukui calculation.
6. Software: `xtb` via chemkit backend (version as provided by chemkit environment).
7. Convergence & warnings: xtb SCF cycles converged for N, N+1, and N–1 states. Engine issued two informational warnings about charge conservation (see below); integrity check marks result as trustworthy.

Reproducibility
• Command executed (from result JSON):
  /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb /Users/evane/Desktop/Research/Argonne/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz --out <live>.json
• Live engine log was streamed to a .out file announced at launch (path available in session history) and contains full SCF and numerical details.
• Generated Fukui data and plot file: …/20260625-114759_ferrocene_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png

Headline result
Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): Fe atom (index 11) with f⁺ = 0.22885459
